# dataland — open dataset registry for AI in Latin America

This site is a referential registry of datasets, especially LATAM-language
data. There is no backend. The entire registry is one static JSON file:

  https://datahub.lat/data.json

## Shape

Top-level keys declare the **vocabularies** (the allowed values for every
constrained field). `records` is a flat array of dataset entries that
reference those vocabularies.

  - tasks_supported            verbs the dataset trains/evaluates (e.g. "transcribe")
  - input_type                 mediums consumed (e.g. "audio")
  - output_type                mediums produced (e.g. "text")
  - domains                    knowledge groupings (e.g. "general", "medical")
  - languages                  LATAM-first tags; sub-variant codes like "es-AR",
                               coarse "es"/"pt" when sub-variants are unstated,
                               "Multilingual" for multi-language data, "N/A" for
                               non-linguistic
  - licenses                   short-form names (e.g. "CC0", "CC-BY-SA 4.0")
  - contributing_organizations array of { name, logo }

Each entry in `records` has these fields:

  id            int, unique
  task          verb from tasks_supported
  input_type    from input_type
  output_type   from output_type
  domain        from domains
  languages     array of tags from languages (non-empty)
  organization  name from contributing_organizations
  license       from licenses
  model         the dataset's display name
  year          first-release year, int
  source_url    canonical URL to the upstream dataset (HF, Mozilla Data
                Collective, etc.) — this is what you fetch to actually
                get the data
  description   2–3 sentences with quantitative scope

## Querying

There is no query API. To find datasets:

  1. GET https://datahub.lat/data.json
  2. Filter records[] client-side on whatever fields matter to you.
  3. Each surviving record's `source_url` points to the dataset itself.
     Go there to download.

Example — every transcription dataset that covers Argentinian Spanish:

  const r = await fetch('https://datahub.lat/data.json').then(r => r.json())
  const hits = r.records.filter(d =>
    d.task === 'transcribe' && d.languages.includes('es-AR'))
  // hits[i].source_url is the upstream dataset URL

## Contributing

Open registry. To add a dataset, open a PR against:

  https://github.com/surus-lat/dataland

The `url-to-dataset-record` skill (in Claude Code) drafts records from
HuggingFace and Mozilla Data Collective URLs.
